song_gekka_no_yasoukyoku.notes: Major-label single released 1998-02-11 (CODA-1415). v2 entity for graph linking.

album_merveilles.notes: Full 12-track listing synced from Discogs (master 558472). v2 song entities exist for key tracks; remaining album tracks are in v1 song catalog.

title.original: ミュージックステーション

appearance_1995_12_music_station.notes: Suspected major-debut TV period (1st single 麗しき仮面の招待状 released 1995-12-10). M-Station back-number archives checked 2026-07: no Malice Mizer entry found for 1995. Do not link 月下の夜想曲 — that single was released 1998-02-11. Exact date and setlist TBC.

appearance_1996_heys_music_champ.notes: Stub — Fuji TV on-air guest lists for 1996 checked 2026-07 with no Malice Mizer entry found. Confirm date, setlist, and footage before promotion.

appearance_1996_pop_jam_interview.notes: Stub — Pop Jam is an NHK music program (1995–2006), not TV Asahi. No primary source confirms a Malice Mizer appearance; setlist and date TBC.

facts.statement: TV special broadcast December 1997 with tour footage (Akasa BLITZ, Hibiya outdoor), member segments, and Verte Aile interview material.

appearance_1997_12_limage_de_merveilles.notes: Footage later issued on La Collection "merveilles" box DVD (2005). Japanese Wikipedia lists under テレビ番組; Weblio corroborates.

facts.statement: Regular CBC Radio show hosted by Gackt, Kami, and Közi; aired Sundays 24:00–24:30. Episode #5 documented 1997-11-09 (MIsaO Lab transcript).

appearance_1997_nhk_radio_interview.notes: Entity id retained from earlier NHK FM stub. Program is CBC Radio (Chukyo), not NHK. Ran through October 1998 (~51 episodes per fan chronologies).

title.original: Beast of Blood — CDTVパフォーマンス

facts.statement: Beast of Blood single charted on CDTV weekly ranking (35th, 2001-06-30) during promotion for the 2001-06-21 Midi:Nette release.
facts.notes: CDTV chart data via cd100.net; not a confirmed CM spot.

appearance_1999_beast_of_blood_cm.notes: Entity id retained from earlier CM stub (incorrectly dated 1999-04). Beast of Blood was released 2001-06-21, not 1999. No TV commercial confirmed; CDTV chart week used as best available broadcast tie-in.

title.original: 薔薇の肖像「新たなる悪意と悲劇の幕開け」
title.romanized: Bara no Shouzou — Shinataru akui to higeki no makuake
title.english: Portrait of Roses — A New Malice and Tragedy Begins

facts.statement: TV Tokyo network special aired 2000-07-30 25:20–25:50; part of the 薔薇の軌跡 television series documented on Oricon artist profile.

appearance_2000_07_bara_no_kiseki_tv.notes: Third-period lineup (Klaha era). Related home video 薔薇の軌跡 released 2001-04-25.

bio.before: Performed as drummer and vocalist in Cains:Feel and other Okinawa-area bands before being recruited as Malice Mizer's second vocalist.
bio.during: Joined in October 1995 and fronted the band through its commercial peak. The Gackt era brought French pop influences, major-label success (Merveilles went gold), and elaborate theatrical live shows. Departed in January 1999 at the height of the band's popularity.
bio.after: Launched an enormously successful solo career spanning music, film, and television. Remains one of Japan's best-known entertainers and declined invitations to Malice Mizer reunion concerts.

bio.before: Began in the scene as a roadie for YOUTHQUAKE, then drummed in DATURA — an obscure early-1990s Japanese visual kei band (not the Eurodance or metal acts of the same name) — which released a self-titled demo tape before disbanding.
bio.during: Malice Mizer's founding drummer, joining Mana, Közi, Yu~ki, and Tetsu in August or September 1992. Recorded drums on Sans Logique (October 1992) and the pre-Kami sessions behind Sadness and related early demos; Lafflesia is the only early demo without their playing. Also appeared on the 1993 indies compilation Brain Trash via "Speed of Desperate". Left after their final show on March 15, 1993, swapping straight with Kneuklid Romance drummer Kami. Briefly supported Baiser on drums from December 1992 to October 1993, overlapping with Malice Mizer.
bio.after: Joined Kneuklid Romance in May 1993, first as support and from 1994 as an official member in the G. Kenichi / B. Tatsuya / D. Gaz lineup. Remained through the band's indies and major-label period until leaving in 1998. Later played in the Night Breeze Zinnia, Doverman (ドーベルマン), and BUZZ BONE, with additional support stints in Virgin Killer 5, The Veletta, and Pandemic. Returned for Kneuklid Romance reunion performances in 2009, 2011, and 2012. Died on December 22, 2017, from cancer. Mana announced the news publicly the next day; the funeral was private. On July 8, 2018, surviving Kneuklid Romance members and friends held the memorial concert GAZ presents "Chain" at Meguro Rockmaykan.

person_gaz.notes: Blood type O; about 168 cm / 52 kg (malice-archive). Shares a birthday (February 1, 1972) with Kami. Career summary cross-checked with visualkei.fandom.com, vk.gy, and malice-archive.neocities.org.

bio.before: Drummed in the punk band 3PMayonnaise during high school (1989–1991), then moved to Tokyo and joined Kneuklid Romance in September 1992.
bio.during: Swapped bands with Gaz and joined Malice Mizer on March 17, 1993. Drummed through the Gackt era and the band's major-label peak. After their death they were credited as "eternal blood relative" on all subsequent releases.
bio.after: Died of a subarachnoid hemorrhage on June 21, 1999, aged 27. Left behind unfinished compositions released on the Shinwa EP and memorial releases.

gallery_images.caption: Cher de Memoire tour final, Meguro Rock Meikan (1994-12-27)

bio.before: Co-founded the new wave band Pride of Mind as vocalist and lyricist, releasing one album before the group disbanded in 1996. Disappeared from the music scene for several years.
bio.during: Invited by Yu~ki to sing on Bara no Seidou (2000). Adopted the name Klaha and became official vocalist after the Budokan shows in September 2000. Fronted the band's final gothic era until the indefinite hiatus in December 2001.
bio.after: Started a solo pop career (Nostal Lab, 2002; Märchen, 2003; Setsubou, 2004). Last performed in April 2004 and has since withdrawn from public life. Did not respond to reunion invitations for the 2018 anniversary concerts.

bio.before: Started in Beyond the Rein Sight (1989) and Girl before joining Matenrou with Mana in 1991. Left with Mana to pursue their shared twin-guitar concept.
bio.during: Co-founded Malice Mizer in August 1992. Composed several of the band's biggest singles, including "Gekka no Yasoukyoku" and "Illuminati", and developed the signature dual-guitar arrangements with Mana.
bio.after: Formed Eve of Destiny, pursued a solo career, and led bands including Dalle, XA-VAT, and ZIZ. Reunited with Mana and Yu~ki for Deep Sanctuary and Malice Mizer anniversary performances.

gallery_images.caption: Cher de Memoire tour final, Meguro Rock Meikan (1994-12-27)

bio.before: Began in underground bands Ves.tearge (1986) and Girl'e as guitarist Serina (1988–1990). Joined Matenrou as bassist alongside Közi before leaving in April 1992.
bio.during: Co-founded Malice Mizer in August 1992. Served as lead guitarist, chief songwriter, choreographer, and artistic director. Founded the Midi:Nette label and shaped the band's classical and gothic visual identity through every lineup change until the 2001 hiatus.
bio.after: Launched the solo project Moi dix Mois (2002), runs the Moi-même-Moitié fashion label and Midi:Nette. Has reunited with Közi and Yu~ki for Deep Sanctuary concerts, including Malice Mizer's 25th-anniversary shows in 2018.

gallery_images.caption: Cher de Memoire tour final, Meguro Rock Meikan (1994-12-27)

bio.before: Briefly performed with Ner-vous in 1992 before becoming Malice Mizer's founding vocalist when the band formed in August that year.
bio.during: Fronted the band's first era (1992–1994), shaping its early gothic and classical sound. Sang on Memoire and numerous demos and compilations. Left after their final show on December 27, 1994, unwilling to pursue the visual kei direction the other members wanted.
bio.after: Moved toward straight rock with Mega8Ball (1995–1997), the supergroup Zigzo (1999–2002), and their primary band nil (2002–present). Also active in The JuneJulyAugust, The Black Comet Club Band, and Disco Volante via their label Afro Skull Records.

gallery_images.caption: Cher de Memoire tour final, Meguro Rock Meikan (1994-12-27)

bio.before: Played bass in the visual kei band Ze:ro (as Yû-KI) until the group disbanded in June 1992, shortly before Malice Mizer formed.
bio.during: Recruited by Mana and Közi as founding bassist in August 1992. One of only three members present for the band's entire nine-year run. Known for their aristocratic/vampire stage persona and composition of "Syunikiss" on Merveilles.
bio.after: Has largely stepped back from music since 2001. Wrote lyrics for Közi's 2004 single "Memento". Appeared at Deep Sanctuary reunion shows from 2010 onward and later sold handcrafted jewelry through Midi:Nette.

org_cbc.notes: Host of MALICE MIZER no Mayonaka no s'il vous plaît (真夜中のs'il vous plaît), Sunday 24:00–24:30 (per malice-mizer.info).

org_nhk.notes: Public broadcaster; radio and television appearances.

org_ntv.notes: Broadcaster of HEY! HEY! HEY! MUSIC CHAMP among other music programs.

org_tv_asahi.notes: Broadcaster of Music Station (ミュージックステーション).

org_tv_tokyo.notes: Oricon TV listing for 薔薇の肖像 special (2000-07-30).

ref_malice_mizer_info.title: MALICE MIZER Info
ref_malice_mizer_info.notes: Chronology, release dates, and media appearances cross-checked against official Columbia/Midi:Nette sources where noted on site.

ref_shoxx_061.title: SHOXX Vol. 61
ref_shoxx_061.notes:
MIsaO Lab (1998/03 vol.061): joint La'cryma Christi cover; カラー38p; パート別対談、Q&A（衣装：au revoir）. "au revoir" names the costume theme for the 38pp 聖邪の共宴 session — not magazine pages 12–13. TOC (gallery file 02) lists feature pp. 16–53: five SESSION pairings (018/024/030/036/042) plus Q&A.
Scan status (July 2026): 40 MM pages on The Malice Archive / file.garden (Anna scan; cover by Morgianasama). Gallery file index ≠ printed page numbers (e.g. gallery 12–13 are solo portraits, not mag. pp. 12–13). Page mapping and Reddit crop matches: scripts/research/shoxx_061_scan_hunt.yaml#page_mapping.
Full issue RAR (Eternal Silence 2009, 84 JPEGs) re-upload not found. Cantavanda has May 1998 vol.63 only.
